customer_anxiety_profile: Blair is not solar-anxious — they are process-anxious. The injury is institutional: a prior rep promised a callback, the dialer called five more times instead, and Blair called back to find out what was wrong. The appointment was booked to stop the calls, not out of curiosity. Blair's show-rate risk is compliance fatigue, not objection.
unresolved_concerns: Broken promise from prior rep — someone told Blair they would call back; that specific commitment was never acknowledged by Matt, only the general call volume was apologized for.
Blair's availability is hedged — 'I should be free' at 01:37 is not a confirmed commitment. Blair may not treat Monday at 10AM as a hard appointment.
No curiosity was created around the prior solar design — Blair has no pull reason to attend beyond 'calls will stop.'
sensitive_topics_raised: Repeated unsolicited contact — Blair counted six calls and named five post-promise calls specifically.
Broken institutional promise — prior rep said they would call back; the system did not honor that.
exact_promises_rep_made: Matt promised to stop the dialer calls: 'I'll get that all taken care of for you.'
Matt confirmed the appointment for Monday at 10AM.
Matt said a text would be sent beforehand to reconfirm the appointment.
closer_should_know: Blair booked to stop the calls, not because they are interested in solar. The closer must open by acknowledging the prior design specifically ('We put this together for your address — let me show you what the numbers look like') to give Blair a reason to stay on the call. If the closer opens generically, Blair has no pull reason to engage and will disengage quickly. Also: the broken-promise injury was never fully repaired — if Blair mentions it, acknowledge it specifically before moving to the design.
